message:
perf(btree): bounds-elide BtreePageHeader::parse hot path

`BtreePageHeader::parse` is the canonical entry on every cursor
`load_page` (and again on every `reload_page_fresh`, balance walk,
and most downstream cursor scaffolding); MT 8t mt-mvcc-bench shows
it at 0.51% self-time as a separate symbol because the original
`pub fn parse` was not `#[inline]`d and the eight `h[i]` accesses
after `let h = &page[header_offset..]` each took a runtime
bounds-check (LLVM could not propagate the `saturating_sub`-based
guard through the unsized slice).

Replace the unsized slice with a single bounds-checked array
conversion:

    let h: &[u8; 8] = page.get(off..off + 8).and_then(|s| s.try_into().ok())?;

After this, every `h[0]..h[7]` access is statically in-bounds and
LLVM elides the bounds-check; the interior-page tail is pulled out
the same way as a separate `&[u8; 4]`. Add `#[inline]` to `parse`,
to the `parse_page_header` wrapper, and to `header_offset_for_page`
so the cross-crate hot callers in `cursor.rs` and `balance.rs` see
both the elided body and the constant `header_offset = 0` (every
non-page-1 caller).

Microbench (`bench_parse_page_header`, mixed leaf+interior pages,
n=4_000_000, 5 trials, --release):

    optimized:   3.47 – 4.03 ns/parse
    naive:      10.59 – 10.97 ns/parse
    delta:      -63.3% to -67.2%  (~2.9x speedup)

Naive baseline mirrors the pre-change shape (per-byte indexing,
`#[inline(never)]`) so the delta isolates the bounds-elision +
header-offset folding combination.

Functional behavior unchanged — corruption errors still surface
the same `remaining`/`offset` detail strings and the same
"page too small for interior B-tree header" / zero-right-child
diagnostics. All 366 fsqlite-btree --release lib tests that pass
on baseline still pass; the one pre-existing
`prop_index_btree_structural_invariants_hold_after_random_mutations`
failure is unchanged on both branches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
